name: HNMS O 10 (P 10)
events: The history of HrMs O 10 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine.
This page was last updated in March 2016.

10 May 1940 In the early morning hours, HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)), departed Den Helder to patrol off the Dutch coast. At 0600 hours a radio message is received that Holland is at war with Germany. O 10 remains on patrol. (1)
11 May 1940 Late in the evening HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) enters Den Helder harbour. (1)
12 May 1940 In the evening, HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)), departed Den Helder together with HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN) to make for England. They were escorted by the tug Witte Zee. (1)
14 May 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at the Downs. (1)

15 May 1940 HrMs Willem van der Zaan (Lt. Cdr. H.D. Lindner RNN), HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN), HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)), HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G. Koudijs, RNN) and HrMs O 24 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN) all departed the Downs for Porstmouth where they arrived the next day. (1)
29 May 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Portsmouth for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the English Channel to the south of the Isle of Wight.
For the daily positions of HrMs O 10 during this patrol see the map below.
1 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) ended her 1st war patrol at Portland.
At Portland she is assigned to A/S training duties. (2)
5 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Fernie (Lt.Cdr. R.McC.P. Jonas, RN) and HMS Gardenia (Lt.Cdr. T.A.O. Ellis, RD, RNR). (1)
8 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Fernie (Lt.Cdr. R.McC.P. Jonas, RN) and HMS Gardenia (Lt.Cdr. T.A.O. Ellis, RD, RNR). (1)
9 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Ambuscade (Lt.Cdr. A.O. Johnson, RN) and the French trawler Asie. (1)
13 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Heartsease (Lt.Cdr. E.J.R. North, RNR). (1)
15 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Heartsease (Lt.Cdr. E.J.R. North, RNR) and two minesweepers. (1)
20 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Ambuscade (Lt.Cdr. A.O. Johnson, RN) and HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). (3)
21 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Valena (Capt. W.H. Poole, RNR). (3)
22 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Ambuscade (Lt.Cdr. A.O. Johnson, RN) and HMS Hambledon (Cdr. S.H. Carlill, RN). (3)
24 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Hurricane (Lt.Cdr. H.C. Simms, RN) and HMS Olvina (Lt.Cdr. C.G. Cuthbertson, RNR). (3)
25 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Valena (Capt. W.H. Poole, RNR). (3)
26 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Foylebank (Capt.(Retd.) H.P. Wilson, RN) and HMS Hambledon (Cdr. S.H. Carlill, RN). (3)
27 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN). (3)
30 Jun 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Olvina (Lt.Cdr. C.G. Cuthbertson, RNR) and HMS Geranium (T/Lt. A. Foxall, RNR). (3)
1 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Geranium (T/Lt. A. Foxall, RNR). (3)
2 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Geranium (T/Lt. A. Foxall, RNR) and HMS St. Modwen (Cdr.(retired) W.A. Ford, RN). (3)
3 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Valena (Capt. W.H. Poole, RNR). (3)
4 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Conqueror (Capt.(Retd.) E.T.R. Chambers, RN). (3)
5 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Portland together with HMS Conqueror (Capt.(Retd.) E.T.R. Chambers, RN). (3)

9 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Portland for Rothesay. She made the passage together with HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) and HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN). This later submarine was coming from Portsmouth.
The submarines were escorted by the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) during this passage. (3)

11 Jul 1940 The Dutch submarines HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN), HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)), HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN) and their escort the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) arrive at Milford Haven. (3)

14 Jul 1940 The Dutch submarines HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN), HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)), HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN) and their escort the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) arrive at Rothesay. (3)
19 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) was docked at Elderslie. (3)
27 Jul 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) was undocked at Elderslie. She then returned to Rothesay. (3)
1 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted practice attacks on HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN) in the Clyde area. (3)
2 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (3)
3 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (3)

4 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. John J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN), HMS Carina (Cdr.(Retd.) T.St.V.F. Tyler, RN) and HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN). (3)
5 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr. (Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN). (3)
6 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) and HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN). (3)

7 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Topaze (Lt.Cdr. John J.N. Hambly, MBE, RN) and HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN). (3)
8 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr. (Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). (3)
9 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN). (3)

10 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Capt.(Retd.) E.C. Stubbs, RN) and HMS Lady Sharazad (Cdr.(Retd.) A.G. Venables, RN). Upon completion of these exercises she shifted from the Campbeltown area to Rothesay. (3)
17 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (3)

21 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) departed Rothesay for Dundee. She made the passage together with the British submarine HrMs Tuna (Lt.Cdr. M.K. Cavenagh-Mainwaring, RN) that was to proceed to Rosyth. They were escorted by the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN). (3)
26 Aug 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)) arrived at Dundee where she was taken in hand for refit. (3)
2 Oct 1940 During her refit, HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. G. Quint, RNN(R)), was docked at Dundee. (3)
29 Nov 1940 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) was undocked. She then continued her refit. (3)
11 Jan 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) conducted a diving trial off Dundee. A leak was detected near the engine room hatch. (3)
27 Jan 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) conducted trials off Dundee. (3)
1 Feb 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) continued her trials off Dundee. Much problems were experienced with the engine room cover hatch. (3)
22 Feb 1941 With her trials completed, HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN), departed Dundee for Rothesay. She made the passage in a convoy. (3)

25 Feb 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) suffers from engine failure. She parted with the convoy and made for Tobermory escorted by HMS Pentland Firth (Skr. H.C. Aisthorpe, RNR) where she arrived late on this day. (3)
28 Feb 1941 After engine repairs, HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN), departed Tobermory for Rosyth escorted by HMS Pentland Firth (Skr. H.C. Aisthorpe, RNR). (3)
1 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Rosyth. (3)
3 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included several practice attacks on HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (3)
4 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included several practice attacks on HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (3)
5 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included several practice attacks on HMS Oberon (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Pizey, DSO, RN). (3)
6 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included several practice attacks on HMS Oberon (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Pizey, DSC, RN). (3)
8 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Rothesay for Londonderry via Holyhead. She was escorted by HMS Walker (Cdr. D.G.F.W. MacIntyre, RN). (3)
9 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Holyhead. (3)
10 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Holyhead for Londonderry. She is escorted by HMS Winchelsea (Lt.Cdr. W.A.F. Hawkins, DSC, RN). (3)
11 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Londonderry. (3)
12 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Bluebell (Lt.Cdr. R.E. Sherwood, RNR). (3)
14 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Philante (Capt.(Retd.) H.S. Bowlby, RN). (3)
15 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with aircraft. (3)

16 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Salisbury (Lt.Cdr. H.M.R. Crichton, RN) and HMS Gladiolus (Lt.Cdr. H.M.C. Sanders, DSC, RNR). (3)
17 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Man o' War (T/Lt. W.W. Creber, RNR) and HMS Northern Dawn (Lt. G.P.S. Lowe, RNVR). (3)

18 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Douglas (Cdr. W.E. Banks, DSC, RN), HMS Man o' War (T/Lt. W.W. Creber, RNR) and HMS Chesterfield (Lt.Cdr. E. Gleave, RNR). (3)
24 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Londonderry for Falmouth. She was escorted by HMS St. Zeno (T/Lt. J.K. Craig, RNVR). (3)
26 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Falmouth. (4)
29 Mar 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Falmouth for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.
5 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) ended her 2nd war patrol at Plymouth. (5)

11 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Plymouth for Rothesay. She made the passage together with HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) and HMS H 33 (Lt. C.H. Rankin, RN). They were escorted by the Free French minesweeper La Moqueuse. (4)
14 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Rothesay. (4)
18 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Rothesay for Londonderry. She was escorted by HMS Wellard (Lt.(Retd.) R.F. Turnbull RD, RNR). (4)
19 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Londonderry. (4)

22 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Sabre (Lt. Sir P.W. Gretton, DSC, RN), HMS Dianella (T/Lt. J.G. Rankin, RNR), HMS Kingcup (Lt. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMS Sunflower (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Jones, RNR). (4)

23 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Keppel (Cdr. A.M. Sheffield, RN), HMS Douglas (Cdr. W.E. Banks, DSC, RN), HMS Roxborough (Lt. V.A. Wight-Boycott, OBE, RN), HMS Gladiolus (Lt.Cdr. H.M.C. Sanders, DSC, RNR), HMS Veronica (Lt.Cdr. (retired) D.F. White, RNR), HMS St. Elstan (T/Lt. G. Butcher, RNVR) and HMS Vizalma (T/Lt. M.M. Firth, RNVR). (4)
24 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Egret (A/Capt. E.M. Haes, RN) and HMS Verbena (Lt.Cdr. D.A. Rayner, DSC, RNVR). (4)
25 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with aircraft. (4)
28 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with aircraft. (4)
29 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with aircraft. (4)
30 Apr 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Clarkia (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Jones, RNR). (4)

1 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Newmarket (Lt.Cdr. G.B. O'Brien Harding, RN) and HMS Clarkia (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Jones, RNR). (4)
2 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with aircraft. (4)

6 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Kingcup (Lt. R.A.D. Cambridge, RNR), HMS Alisma (A/Lt.Cdr. M.G. Rose, RANVR), HMS Sabre (Lt. Sir P.W. Gretton, DSC, RN), HMS Keppel (Cdr. A.M. Sheffield, RN), HMS Fleetwood (Cdr. R.W. Moir, RN) and HMS Lincoln (Lt. R.J. Hanson, RN). (4)
7 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Vizalma (T/Lt. M.M. Firth, RNVR) and HMS Salisbury (Lt.Cdr. H.M.R. Crichton, RN). (4)

8 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Burnham (Cdr. J. Bostock, DSC, RN), HMS Salisbury (Lt.Cdr. H.M.R. Crichton, RN), HMS Eridge (Lt.Cdr. W.F.N. Gregory-Smith, RN), HMS St. Elstan (T/Lt. G. Butcher, RNVR), HMS Vizalma (T/Lt. M.M. Firth, RNVR), HMS Gladiolus (Lt.Cdr. H.M.C. Sanders, DSC, RNR) and HMS Veronica (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) D.F. White, RNR). (4)

9 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Roxborough (Lt. V.A. Wight-Boycott, OBE, RN), HMS St. Elstan (T/Lt. G. Butcher, RNVR) and HMS St. Zeno (T/Lt. J.K. Craig, RNVR). (4)
11 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Skate (Lt. F.P. Baker, DSC, RN) and HMS Londonderry (Cdr. J.S. Dalison, RN). (4)
12 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Saladin (Lt.Cdr. L.J. Dover, RN) and HMS Sunflower (Lt.Cdr. J.T. Jones, RNR). (4)

15 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Sherwood (Lt.Cdr. S.W.F. Bennetts, RN) and HMS Dianella (T/Lt. J.G. Rankin, RNR) and aircraft.
In the early evening HrMs O 10 was hit by a practice bomb that damaged the pressure hull. The exercise was then cancelled and O 10 proceeded to Londonderry for repairs. (4)

18 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Beagle (Lt.Cdr. R.T. White, DSO and Bar, RN) and HMCS Columbia (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) S.W. Davis, RN). (4)

19 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMCS Columbia (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) S.W. Davis, RN) and HMCS St. Clair (Lt.Cdr. D.C. Wallace, RCNR). (4)
20 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMCS Eyebright (T/Lt. E. Randell, RCNR) and HMS Carnation (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Houchen, OBE, RNR). (4)
21 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMCS Eyebright (T/Lt. E. Randell, RCNR) and HMS Carnation (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Houchen, OBE, RNR). (4)

22 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMCS Eyebright (T/Lt. E. Randell, RCNR), HMS Carnation (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Houchen, OBE, RNR) and HMS Lady Madeleine (T/Lt. W.G. Ogden, RNVR). (4)
24 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) departed Londonderry for Rothesay. She is escorted by HMS Northern Dawn (Lt. G.P.S. Lowe, RNVR). (4)
25 May 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) arrived at Rothesay. (4)
2 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. (4)
3 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Valena (Lt.Cdr. A.F.C. Gray, RNR) and HMS L' Atlantique (Skr. A. McKay, RNR). (4)
4 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) N.T.B. Holt, RN) and HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN). (4)
5 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Tuscarora (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) N.T.B. Holt, RN) and HMS Spaniel (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (4)
6 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Valena (Lt.Cdr. A.F.C. Gray, RNR) and HMS L' Atlantique (Skr. A. McKay, RNR). (4)

7 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS St. Modwen (Cdr.(Retd.) W.A. Ford, RN) and HMS L' Atlantique (Skr. A. McKay, RNR). Upon completion of these exercises HrMs O 10 proceeded to Rothesay for engine repairs. (4)

11 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) shifted from Rothesay to the Campbeltown area where she participated in A/S exercises together with HMS Tuscarora (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) N.T.B. Holt, RN) and HMS Spaniel (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (4)
12 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS St. Modwen (Cdr.(retired) W.A. Ford, RN) and HMS Spaniel (Skr. C. Coultas, DSC, RNR). (4)
13 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Valena (Lt.Cdr. A.F.C. Gray, RNR) and HMS Bedlington (Skr. J.H.D. Dansie, RNR). (4)
15 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Boarhound (Skr. S.G. Jinks, RNR) and HMS Bedlington (Skr. J.H.D. Dansie, RNR). (4)
16 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS Shemara (Cdr.(Retd.) H. Buckle, RN) and HMS Tuscarora (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) N.T.B. Holt, RN). (4)
17 Jun 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. J.H. Geijs, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Campbeltown with HMS St. Modwen (Cdr.(Retd.) W.A. Ford, RN) and HMS Valena (Lt.Cdr. A.F.C. Gray, RNR). (4)

16 Dec 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) shifted from Campbeltown to Rothesay. She made the passage together with HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN). They were escorted by HMS L' Atlantique (Skr. A. McKay, RNR).

Later on the same day she departed Rothesay for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay. Passage south through the Irish Sea was made together with HMS H 31 (Lt. F.B. Gibbs, RN) and HMS H 34 (Lt. W.A. Phillimore, RN). They were escorted by HMS Boarhound (Skr. S.G. Jinks, RNR) until 0700/17 when HMS Alecto (Cdr. R.M.G. Gambier, RN) took over the escort until 0500/19 while off Wolf Rock.
23 Dec 1941 Around 0810 A/23, HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) made rendezvous near Bishops Rock with HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN).
HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) was also to join but she only arrived at the rendezvous around 1100 A/23.
In the meantime HrMs O 21 had carried out three A/S exercises with HMS Cutty Sark.
On HrMs O 10 joining they set course for Milford Haven. (8)

18 May 1942 HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) departed Lerwick for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Norwegian Sea to provide cover during convoy operations to and from Northern Russia (convoys PQ 16 / QP 12).

25 May 1942 In signals timed 1628 B/25 and 1712 B/25, the Flag Officer Submarines sent the following orders to HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN), HMS P 614 (Lt. D.J. Beckley, RN), HNoMS Uredd (Lt. R.Q. Røren), HMS P 37 (Lt. H. Winter, RN) and HMS P 46 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN); At 0800 B/26, O 10, P 614, Uredd, P 37 and P 46 proceed so as to be in zones 'K 26', 'K 28', 'K 30', 'K 32' and 'K 34' respectively by 2000 B/26. (10)

27 May 1942 In signals timed 1136 B/27, the Flag Officer Submarine sent the following orders to HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN), HMS P 614 (Lt. D.J. Beckley, RN), HNoMS Uredd (Lt. R.Q. Røren), HMS P 37 (Lt. H. Winter, RN) and HMS P 46 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN); At 0600 B/28, O 10, P 614, Uredd, P 37 and P 46 are to leave their patrol zones and return to Lerwick as will be ordered by the Capt. S.9.

In a signal timed 1741 B/9, the Capt. S.9 sent the following orders to the submarines; Leave patrol at 0600 B/28 for position 083° - Out Skerries - 6 miles, thence Lerwick routed as follows. 1) O 10 and P 614 direct. 2) Uredd through position 70°02'N, 08°25'E. 3) P 37 through position 69°24'N, 08°30'E. 4) P 46 through position 69°15'N, 08°10'E. The submarines are to proceed on the surface at the Commanding Officers discretion and are to adjust speed so as to arrive at Lerwick as follows; O 10 1500 B/31. P 614 2000 B/31. Uredd 0400 B/1 P 37 1100 B/1. P 46 1800 B/1. (10)

13 May 1943 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) developed serious engine defects. The port air compressor malfunctioned resulting in the port diesel engine being inoperative. O 10 reported this and she was ordered to return to Lerwick. Shortly afterwards however the starboard air compressor also malfunctioned. O 10 now was without diesel propulsion. O 10 then continued towards Lerwick submerged. (2)

14 May 1943 At 0340 hours, HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN), was spotted by a Catalina that was sent out to search for her. Around 0600 hours HMS Chiddingfold (Lt. T.M. Dorrien-Smith, RN) came in sight. Late in the morning they were joined by HMS Brecon (Lt.Cdr. T.D. Herrick, DSC and Bar, RN), HMS Brissenden (Lt. D.C. Beatty, RN) and HMS Celia (T/Lt. L.B. Merrick, RNR). HMS Brecon then took O 10 in tow. Around 1600 hours HMS Halcyon (Lt.Cdr. C.H. Corbet-Singleton, RN) arrived which took over the tow. (2)
16 May 1943 HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) ended her 6th war patrol when she arrived at Lerwick. (2)
